link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たなはたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}}[[Category:qq]]

In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:七夕]]

link|Q11430658}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年8月19日 (火) 16:45 (UTC)}} {{神社 |画像=[[画像:Tanabata-Jinja.jpg|270px|Tanabata Shrine, shrine building (2008)]]
Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:Tanabata]]

It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. Torii and haiden |名称=Tanabata Shrine |所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kinjo (Nagoya)|Q60997795|WD=Q60997795|lt=Kinjo|1=Kinjō}} 4-13-16 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
7th grade |創建= Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}}) |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Tendō
Tanabata Forest
Hachiman Shrine |例祭= August 7 |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Tanabata
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanabata Shrine''' (たなばたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kinjo 4-chōme, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207964|WD=Q1207964|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Tanabata Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}, it was called '''Tendō'''. It is said that in the Sengoku period, during a punitive burning by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, the shrine buildings and transmitted documents were burned, so the founding year is unknown. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Tabata village... Tendō... Bizen exemption." It is also said that when {{ill|lt=Nagoya Castle|Q648629|WD=Q648629|1=Nagoya Castle}} was built, the shrine was moved to its current site by land substitution. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} mentioned a theory that in earlier times it was located in "Ofuke no kaku-nai" (possibly in present {{ill|lt=Meijō Park|Q6810044|WD=Q6810044|1=Meijō Park}}). Around the current shrine is the Tanabatachō archaeological site where {{ill|lt=Yayoi pottery|Q11487461|WD=Q11487461|1=Yayoi pottery}} has been excavated. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it came under the control of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Tōshō-gū|Q3335117|WD=Q3335117|1=Nagoya Tōshō-gū}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era new shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. On the annual festival day of the old lunar {{ill|lt=July 7|Q839741|WD=Q839741|lt=7th day of the 7th month|1=July 7 (Lunisolar)}}, the domain lord visited and ordinary townspeople were also permitted to worship inside the enclosure; many records survive, including depictions in the {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|WD=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}. After it was identified in the Edo period with Yamada District's "Tanabata Shrine" in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, this became the accepted theory. In the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} it appears as "Junior Third Rank Upper, Keta-hata Tenjin," suggesting the shikinaisha Tanabata Shrine was also called Keta-hata Tenjin. The main reason this shrine was identified as Tanabata Shrine is that it was located in Tabata Village of {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, with similar village and shrine names. The {{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}} identifies Tanabata Shrine with Sansha Myōjin in {{ill|lt=Obata (Nagoya)|Q52158082|WD=Q52158082|lt=Obata Village|1=Obata}}, but late-Edo gazetteers including {{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|WD=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}} consistently identify this shrine as Tanabata Shrine. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, when the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was implemented, it became a village shrine under the name Hachiman Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|WD=Q2165|1=1922}}) it became a designated village shrine. During {{ill|lt=World War II|Q362|WD=Q362|1=World War II}}, an {{ill|lt=anti-aircraft gun|Q21156889|WD=Q21156889|1=anti-aircraft gun}} battery was placed on shrine grounds, and much of the shrine forest known as "Tanabata Forest" was cut down. In the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|WD=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}} of May 14, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), the same raid that destroyed Nagoya Castle's main keep, the shrine buildings were burned down. The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|WD=Q2652|1=1964}} (Shōwa 39). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Kami|Q48750893|WD=Q48750893|lt=Ame-no-Tanahata-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Orihime|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijingū}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrines === * Kotohira Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Tsushima Shrine * Yamagami Shrine === Festivals === * Monthly festival: every month on the 7th * Jan 1 - New Year Festival * Jan 14 - Sagichō Festival * Mar 7 - Kinen Festival * Jul 7 - Chinowa Festival * Aug 7 - Annual Grand Festival * Oct 7 - Prayer Festival * Dec 7 - Niiname Festival * Dec 30 - Great Purification Festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|WD=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Meijō Line|Q906775|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from Exit 2 of {{ill|lt=Meijō Kōen Station|Q863874|WD=Q863874|1=Meijō Kōen Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|WD=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jōhokubashi" on Sakae 11 / Sakae 25 routes, about 5 minutes on foot. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|WD=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshi Shrine (Nagoya)|Q11512981|WD=Q11512981|lt=Hoshi Shrine|1=Hoshi Shrine}} - another shrine deeply connected with Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|WD=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|w|518782291}} {{Commonscat}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/kita4.html Walking Nagoya "Yanagihara / Meijō": The Two Who Could Not Meet on Tanabata]{{Ja icon}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市北区の建築物]] [[Category:Tanabata]]
